name: La Atalaya and Cueva Mingubela Loop
name_original: Cueva Mingubela y La Atalaya — circular desde Ojos-Albos
description: This difficult 7.2-mile jogging route from Ojos-Albos climbs 1360 feet, offering rock art at Cueva Mingubela and views from La Atalaya.
region_intro: Jogging around Avila offers a diverse range of routes, from urban paths alongside historical city walls to trails winding through river valleys and natural parks. The region features varied terrain, including flat sections along the Río Adaja and more challenging ascents in the surrounding hills. Runners can explore both the city's ancient architecture and the tranquil landscapes beyond its boundaries. Best jogging routes around Avila The most popular jogging route is City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Adaja River, offering scenic views of the city. Another top favourite among local runners is Adaja River Trail loop from Ávila, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route provides a longer exploration of the riverbanks and surrounding natural areas. Local runners also love the Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila, an 11.0 miles (17.8 km) trail leading through historical cityscapes and surrounding terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Jogging around Avila is defined by river valleys, historical city walls, and natural parks, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Avila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 1160 runners have used komoot to explore Avila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available in Avila?
A: There are over 90 jogging routes around Avila, offering a wide variety of options for all fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails in the surrounding hills.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Avila?
A: The running routes in Avila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, scenic views of the city and river, and the opportunity to combine historical exploration with outdoor activity.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Avila?
A: Avila offers pleasant weather for jogging year-round, thanks to its protected southerly location. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for longer runs, while summer mornings and evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat. Winter can also be great for crisp, clear runs.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes in Avila?
A: Yes, Avila has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) trail that follows the Adaja River, offering scenic views of the city.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in the Avila region?
A: Absolutely. For a more strenuous workout, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159471347/la-atalaya-and-cueva-mingubela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Atalaya and Cueva Mingubela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain and rugged sections. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159404372/source-of-the-adaja-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Adaja River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering a longer distance and considerable ascent.

Q: Are there circular running routes around Avila?
A: Yes, many of Avila's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adaja River Trail loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, both offering beautiful scenery.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the city walls?
A: Yes, you can experience the historical city walls while running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.0-mile (17.8 km) trail that leads through historical cityscapes and surrounding terrain. For a slightly shorter option, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31481787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila – Ávila City Walls loop from Escuela de Policía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting sights can I see while jogging in Avila?
A: Many jogging routes in Avila offer views of historical landmarks and natural beauty. You can run alongside the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls&lt;/a&gt;, pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural parks and river valleys.

Q: Are Avila's running trails dog-friendly?
A: Many of the natural trails and riverside paths around Avila are suitable for running with dogs, especially those away from the immediate city center. Always ensure your dog is on a leash where required and be mindful of local regulations and other trail users. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31453543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Soto Natural Park loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt; offer open spaces that can be enjoyable for you and your canine companion.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging options in Avila?
A: Yes, Avila offers several family-friendly jogging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159323650/city-of-avila-by-the-rio-adaja-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Ávila by the Río Adaja — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, mostly flat path along the river, suitable for families. These routes often provide a gentle introduction to the region's landscapes without being too strenuous.

Q: Where can I find parking near jogging routes in Avila?
A: Many routes starting from within or just outside the city of Avila have accessible parking nearby. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31526786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Escuela Nacional de Policía&lt;/a&gt;, parking is often available in designated areas. When planning your run, check the specific route details on komoot for information on starting points and potential parking facilities.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on Avila's running trails?
A: Avila's running trails offer a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find urban paths alongside ancient city walls, tranquil routes winding through river valleys like the Adaja, and more rugged trails in the surrounding hills and natural parks. Expect a mix of historical architecture, open fields, and natural woodlands.
tips:
Mingubela Cave, also known as Peña Mingubela, is a fascinating rock shelter located near the small village of Ojos Albos in Ávila, Spain. This site is known for its ancient cave paintings, which depict various figures, including warriors, some of whom are shown holding shields or in prayer positions. The cave is divided by a stone column, which adds to its unique structure.

If you are interested in visiting, it is important to note that the cave is only accessible on foot and the path can be quite steep, so it is advisable to bring a flashlight and seek precise directions from locals. Additionally, there are several hiking trails in the area, such as the circular trail from Ojos Albos that includes La Atalaya and Mingubela Cave.
The highest peak in this small range that is located in the Central System and that is covered by a set of windmills that line the ridge of the mountain. This reaches more than 1500 meters in height.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Cueva Mingubela y La Atalaya route is classified as a difficult run. With an elevation gain of over 400 meters and varied terrain, it requires a good level of fitness and is better suited for experienced trail runners looking for a significant physical challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cueva Mingubela y La Atalaya route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of natural paths, rocky sections, and some street segments. You'll encounter about 9.20 km of trail ('senda'), 2.14 km of path ('sendero'), and 382 meters of street ('calle'). The geology includes sandstone, indicating potentially uneven ground, especially around the prominent rock formations near Peña Mingubela.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The most remarkable feature is the Cueva Mingubela Rock Art, a natural rock shelter with prehistoric rock paintings. You'll also pass by La Atalaya, a summit offering great views. From 'Las Cabras' hill, there are notable vistas of the Voltoya River carving through canyons.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cueva Mingubela y La Atalaya trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Ojos-Albos. You can typically find parking within the village itself, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked for visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Cueva Mingubela rock art site?
faqs.answer: While the Cueva Mingubela rock art site is a protected area, the trail itself does not require a permit or entrance fee for access. The rock paintings are preserved with wooden walkways and UV-resistant glass, and you can view them from the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially slippery conditions on rocky sections.

faqs.question: Is the Cueva Mingubela y La Atalaya trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, due to the challenging terrain and the presence of protected natural and historical sites like the Cueva Mingubela rock art, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near sensitive areas and to ensure their safety on uneven ground.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its classification as a 'difficult' jogging route with significant elevation gain and varied terrain, this trail is generally not recommended for families with young children or those looking for a casual outing. It's best suited for adults with good fitness levels.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or shops in Ojos-Albos?
faqs.answer: Ojos-Albos is a small village. While it may have basic amenities, it's best to check locally for current operating hours of any cafes, pubs, or shops. It's always a good idea to bring your own water and snacks, especially for a challenging route like this.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of the valley and surrounding mountains. From the summit of 'Las Cabras' hill, there are particularly notable vistas of the Voltoya River as it carves through canyons, featuring beautiful waterfalls. The village of Ojos Albos itself is nestled in a mountainous region interspersed with cereal fields.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: For an experienced jogger, the route typically takes around 1 hour and 30 minutes to complete. However, given its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation changes, individual times may vary based on fitness level and pace.
